text:
Market today: Risk-off vibes with a real bite. A rare bear-market signal is flashing (67% historical hit rate), Mag Seven momentum just had its fourth-worst stretch in 22 years, and Morgan Stanley is openly comparing the chip trade to silver — a late-cycle crowded bet. That's your entire book under pressure.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 16% and flagged SELL, margin means that hole is deeper than it looks. PLTR is down 13% and also SELL — two large losers demanding action before stops get tested. SPAL is down 17% with a 2x leveraged structure that decays daily — small position but bleeds fast; watch that $19 stop closely. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history; treat it as fragile.

Watch: (1) Whether SMH holds $540 stop — it's your only green chip name and a bellwether for the whole book. (2) That bear-market signal headline — if broad market rolls over, margin amplifies every drop you're already sitting in.

Bottom line: You have two SELL-flagged losers on margin eating capital right now — AVGO and PLTR need a decision today, not tomorrow.
